label: hobbies_and_activities
value: Restoring a 1967 Chevrolet Camaro and home brewing. Looking for ways to manage home brewing expenses within a budget of $500 to $1000 annually.

label: professional_details
value: Leading an IoT sensor integration project at work aimed at improving safety protocols; experimenting with different technologies within a generous budget; seeking advice on presenting project findings to the management team, including strategies to address common challenges such as technical jargon, cost justification, security concerns, scalability, data privacy, implementation timeline and risks, user adoption and training, vendor reliability and support, integration with existing systems, and regulatory compliance.

label: plans_and_intentions
value: Planning another board game night soon, focusing on party games that can keep a big crowd entertained.

label: health_and_wellness_preferences
value: Uses painting and sketching for stress relief; interested in exploring other creative outlets for stress management such as writing, photography, digital art, crafting, music, meditation apps, cooking, reading, virtual tours, and podcasts/audiobooks. Interested in connecting with online communities and forums for sharing tips and inspiration on creative hobbies.

label: activity_and_service_preferences
value: Interested in strategies for cost-effective home brewing, including reducing batch size, efficient ingredient sourcing, DIY solutions, recipe simplification, brewing efficiency, collaborative brewing, budget tracking, and using secondhand equipment. Specifically interested in affordable grains and hops that offer good flavor profiles such as Pale Malt (2-row or 6-row), Crystal/Caramel Malts (CR-20L to CR-60L), Munich Malt, Flaked Oats or Flaked Barley, Cascade, Centennial, Chinook, Simcoe, and Amarillo.
